title: phenax vulgaris
side_effects: Phenax vulgaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Long-term exposure to the plant's chemicals can also cause liver damage and kidney problems. In some cases, improper use of Phenax vulgaris can lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it may interact with medications and exacerbate existing health conditions, such as high blood pressure and diabetes.
